description_long: This house complex features three distinct structures arranged on a rectangular grassy plot, connected by a paved walkway. The central building is the largest, with a steeply pitched red gable roof, white plaster walls, and exposed wooden beams, evoking a traditional European or Tudor style. Flanking the main house are two smaller buildings, each with similar architectural details, including stone foundations and red-tiled roofs. The windows are symmetrically placed, with wooden frames and mullions, and the main entryway is marked by a small set of steps and a prominent wooden door. Stone chimneys rise from each building, adding to the rustic charm. The overall composition suggests a cohesive residential ensemble, likely intended for a small family or communal living.
description: A traditional European-style house complex with three buildings, red gable roofs, white plaster walls, and exposed wooden beams, set on a grassy plot with a paved walkway.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three buildings, the main entryway, and the connecting walkway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the main building and the alignment of the smaller structures.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades and the stone foundations of each building.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the rooflines and the rear entry of the central building.
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with small steps, flanked by windows and a modest overhang.
